loopbacks.reason: Implementer-reported frozen-test defect (ADR-009 route, shift-left before review): TEST-793 and TEST-794 in tests/agent-session-flow.test.ts call pty:kill with object params { id: 'x' }, but F16's frozen wire contract (validateKillParams in src/agent/validate.ts, pinned by 0017's frozen agent-session vectors, mirroring TermhallaApi.ptyKill) takes the bare pane-id STRING - the kill req answers bad-params, no exit evt ever arrives, and the assertions fail against correct production behavior. TEST-795 in tests/agent-stdio-flow.test.ts has the same wrong call shape (unasserted, so it passes today but silently never kills pane b). Fix: the three call sites become req(h, CH.ptyKill, 'a'/'b') / ask(CH.ptyKill, 'b'); no production code change implied - the implementation is NOT condemned (TEST-795 already proves the full flood/stall/recovery path over real stdio).

Implementer-reported frozen-test collision (ADR-009 route): TEST-781 in tests/remote-flow-control.test.ts uses the vector fill(7)/toBe(7), which trips 0009's repo-wide residual sweep TEST-384 (tests/shared/orky-pane-migration.test.ts) whose assembled needle is the literal string 'toBe(7)' with a closed two-file allow-list - exactly the CONV-022 value-pin collision class; the spec-time enumeration greps (ack/window/pause/Object.keys) could not see it. Fix: change the pane-b accounting vector from 7 to 9 units (semantically identical). No production code change implied.

Apply the two verified MEDIUM review findings now rather than deferring (both are 5-line containment/lifecycle fixes in code F18 builds on; no frozen test is touched): FINDING-001 - wrap flow-decision pause/resume application in try/catch routed to stderr diag (parity with the dispatch path's containment); FINDING-002 - a scripted exit deferred by a pause must also stop the fake shell from processing later buffered command lines (closing flag), matching a real pty's post-exit inertness while keeping the queued-data + deferred-exit delivery unchanged.

implementer.reason: Router requested A/B (orky:implementer + codex:codex-rescue) on the networking+determinism concerns, but this driver environment has no parallel agent-dispatch tool and one shared worktree (two concurrent implementers would collide); codex exec has a recorded stall precedent on this box (0016 implement re-entry 3: backgrounded codex sat on stdin and wrote nothing, killed at the 600s bound) and per ADR-025 codex never holds the pipeline. orky:implementer (Claude, driver-inline) ran solo: 8 source/doc artifacts (flow-control.ts module, barrel, 3 backend files, session wiring, 2 feature docs + changelog), one ADR-009 tests loopback en route (TEST-793/794/795 called pty:kill with object params where F16's frozen wire contract takes the bare pane-id string; fixed at the tests phase, freeze re-captured). Codex is reserved for a bounded read-only verifier cross-check at review.
